one_liner: PLTR +0.84% to $116 — new addition, RSI 30 near oversold, 36.6% DCF upside, 20/2 analyst Buy, but 44% below 52-week high at $207 with no confirmed bottom signal yet.

price_action.intraday_story: PLTR opened at $114.91, touched $118.70, and closed at $116.67. Three-session context: June 26 saw 61.4M shares (1.48x) with a strong close at $112.93, June 29 was 45M (1.08x) at $115.70, and today's 35.2M (0.85x) at $116.67 shows declining volume on modest gains — a stable consolidation above the $112 breakout level from June 26.

news_catalyst.headline: Palantir + Nvidia Integration (Nemotron Models) + U.S. Army AI Modernization Contract
news_catalyst.summary: Palantir has secured a key role in U.S. Army modernization efforts, integrating AI data analytics into military operations — a high-margin, long-duration government contract stream. The Nvidia partnership integrating Nemotron models into Palantir's platform enables secure custom AI model training and deployment for government clients, significantly expanding the AIP (Artificial Intelligence Platform) addressable market. The stock's 52-week range of $106–$207 shows significant correction potential — PLTR is currently 44% below its 52-week high. 20 analysts Buy vs. 2 Sell, with an average 12-month target of $182.75 (57% upside).

technical.summary: PLTR is a 'watch and verify' setup: RSI 30 + declining volume + Grade B fundamentals + 20/2 analyst consensus are all constructive, but the 44% drawdown from ATH means the downtrend needs a formal technical bottom before committing.

wyckoff_narrative: No formal Wyckoff events detected. The June 26 session (61.4M, 1.48x) could be interpreted as an Automatic Rally off a prior selling climax (the ATH distribution at $207). The three sessions of declining volume since then are consistent with a testing phase. A Secondary Test of the June low ($106.37) with below-average volume would be the LPS confirmation for a Wyckoff accumulation entry.

valuation.summary: DCF at 36.6% upside with Grade B fundamentals (0.69 score). The $182.75 analyst mean target represents 57% upside. At RSI 30, the entry is at the lower end of the historical valuation range for PLTR.

key_strengths: U.S. Army AI modernization — high-margin, long-duration government revenue
Nvidia Nemotron integration expands AIP market to secure custom AI
20 Buy / 2 Sell — strong analyst consensus at $182.75 avg target
key_concerns: 44% below 52-week high — significant distribution has occurred
Government contract dependency — any procurement delay impacts revenue visibility
RSI 30 but no confirmed bottom signal yet
fundamentals.summary: Grade B with strong government AI tailwinds. The 44% drawdown from ATH reflects the high-multiple correction that hit AI sentiment plays. At RSI 30, the risk/reward is tilting constructive but needs technical confirmation.

risks: 44% below 52-week high — the distribution was significant and the 52-week low at $106.37 could be tested before a proper bottom
No confirmed Wyckoff accumulation signal — RSI 30 alone is not enough to enter without a volume event
Government contract procurement delays are an execution risk for the AIP timeline
